channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Tensions rise in two ceasefires in the Middle East. And, the Navy secretary ousted
item.description: Tensions are rising in the Middle East as shaky ceasefire agreements between the U.S., Israel and Iran, and Lebanon and Israel, are tested. And, the Secretary of the Navy is out of the role.
item.content:encoded:
Tensions are rising in the Middle East as shaky ceasefire agreements between the U.S., Israel and Iran, and Lebanon and Israel, are tested. And, the Secretary of the Navy is out of the role.

(Image credit: Andrew Cabellero-Reynolds)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The U.S. seizes another oil tanker as peace talks with Iran remain in limbo
item.description: President Trump says he ordered the Navy to "shoot and kill any boat" putting mines in the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. ramps up minesweeping there.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump says he ordered the Navy to "shoot and kill any boat" putting mines in the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. ramps up minesweeping there.

(Image credit: Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Two startlingly different views on long-awaited data on America's anti-HIV efforts
item.description: After a year without data, the State Department released figures on PEPFAR, the program launched by George W. Bush and credited with saving millions of lives. How did Trump's aid cuts affect it?
item.content:encoded:
After a year without data, the State Department released figures on PEPFAR, the program launched by George W. Bush and credited with saving millions of lives. How did Trump's aid cuts affect it?

(Image credit: Ben de la Cruz/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Peace talks between U.S. and Iran at a standstill as Trump extends ceasefire
item.description: President Trump has extended the ceasefire, but Iran says it's not enough if the naval blockade is still in place.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has extended the ceasefire, but Iran says it's not enough if the naval blockade is still in place.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Sycophantic AI flatters and suggests you are not to blame
item.description: The AI models and chatbots that we interact with tend to affirm our feelings and viewpoints — more so than people do, with potentially worrisome consequences.
item.content:encoded: The AI models and chatbots that we interact with tend to affirm our feelings and viewpoints — more so than people do, with potentially worrisome consequences.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: How TikTok is driving American expats to Southeast Asia
item.description: Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.
item.content:encoded:
Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.

(Image credit: Linh Pham)
item.dc:creator: Windsor Johnston

item.title: Senate GOP is kickstarting budget reconciliation to fund ICE. Here's how that works
item.description: After a historic partial shutdown of Homeland Security, congressional Republicans are looking to a budgetary tool that could enable them to fund immigration enforcement agencies without Democratic support.
item.content:encoded:
After a historic partial shutdown of Homeland Security, congressional Republicans are looking to a budgetary tool that could enable them to fund immigration enforcement agencies without Democratic support.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: World Press Photo announces Photo of the Year 2026
item.description: The executive director of World Press Photo said this image shows the inconsolable grief of children losing their father in a place built for justice. It is a stark and necessary record of family separation following the U.S. reform policies.
item.content:encoded:
The executive director of World Press Photo said this image shows the inconsolable grief of children losing their father in a place built for justice. It is a stark and necessary record of family separation following the U.S. reform policies.

(Image credit: Carol Guzy)
item.dc:creator: Ava Pukatch

item.title: 4 charts show where money is going in the midterms -- and who has the most cash
item.description: The latest campaign finance reports show Democratic enthusiasm in key House and Senate races, but national Republican groups have far more in the bank to potentially spend down the road.
item.content:encoded:
The latest campaign finance reports show Democratic enthusiasm in key House and Senate races, but national Republican groups have far more in the bank to potentially spend down the road.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: President Trump's popularity is falling. What does it mean for him and the GOP?
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Jonah Goldberg, editor-in-chief of The Dispatch, about President Trump's declining public approval.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Jonah Goldberg, editor-in-chief of The Dispatch, about President Trump's declining public approval.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
